_comment: These are gating thresholds, NOT performance targets. Set them so a
passing run is the steady-state expectation; raise the floor only after
you've moved the actual baseline. Source-of-truth for guidance is
the values below are HARD (gate fails on breach).
Today these are tuned to MOCK-SERVER latencies (mock-server.ts produces
100ms-5s with cube-skewed distributions, per-endpoint multipliers). When
the gate is wired to real-API runs, bump the per-endpoint p95 budgets
and re-tune off 7 days of green-run baselines.

errorRate._note: 1% across all endpoints. Mock server emits 3% by default; we run with 100 requests so noise can reach 1-2 errors. If false-positive rate is annoying, raise to 0.02 and keep the per-endpoint p95 as the real signal.

p95Ms._note: Triage is one short LLM call. README target <3s; mock-server caps ~3000ms via cube-skew on [100, 3000].

Scribe is the slowest path (longest prompt, tool calls). README target <15s for real API; mock caps ~5000ms. Set 6s as the gate to allow some headroom over mock p95 without hiding real-API regressions if/when the gate is re-targeted.
Retrieval + short synthesis. README target <4s. Mock caps ~4000ms via cube-skew on [150, 4000].
